title: Table alignment
text: Use `:---` for left-aligned, `:---:` for centered, and `---:` for right-aligned cells in tables.

title: Quick copy
text: Copy Markdown with the copy button in the editor or copy text from the preview.

title: Switch between documents
text: Use Ctrl+→ and Ctrl+← to quickly navigate through your documents in the history.

title: Insert footnotes
text: Create footnotes with `[^1]` in the text and `[^1]: footnote text` at the end.
example:
This is some text[^1].

[^1]: This is the footnote.

title: Preview presets
text:
Switch between **Scientific**, **Compact**, **Literary**, and **Document**.

- **Document** uses layout rules directly from your Markdown file
- otherwise it falls back to the default document layout from settings
- ideal for print-like preview and PDF export

title: Task lists
text: Create interactive checklists with `- [ ]` for open and `- [x]` for done.
example:
- [ ] Todo
- [x] Done

title: Emoji shortcuts
text: Use `:smile:`, `:heart:`, `:rocket:` for quick emojis.
example: :smile: :heart: :rocket: :+1:

title: Columns layout
text: Create a two-column layout with `:::: columns` and `:::` for each column.
example:
:::: columns
::: column
Left column
:::
::: column
Right column
:::
::::

title: Admonitions
text: Emphasize important content with `::: note`, `::: warning`, `::: danger`, or `::: success`.
example:
::: warning
Caution: Important note!
:::

title: Outline tree
text:
Use the **outline tree view** to move quickly through large documents.

Tip: It is especially useful for long documents with many headings and sub-sections.

title: Definition lists
text:
Create structured definitions with `Term
: Definition`.
example:
Markdown
: Lightweight markup language

HTML
: Hypertext Markup Language

title: Superscript & subscript
text: Use `^superscript^` and `~subscript~` for formulas.
example: H~2~O and E=mc^2^

title: Highlight text
text: Highlight important text with `==highlighted text==`.
example: This is ==very important==!

title: Abbreviations
text: Define abbreviations with `*[HTML]: Hypertext Markup Language` at the end of the document.
example:
HTML is great!

*[HTML]: Hypertext Markup Language

title: Page view
text:
Enable **page view** for a print-like preview with visible page breaks.

It uses document layout rules instead of the normal screen presets, so it is the best final check before PDF export.

title: Page break
text: Insert `---pb---` to force a page break for PDF export.
example:
Page 1

---pb---

Page 2

title: Auto-save
text: Your documents are saved automatically — no fear of data loss!

title: KaTeX math
text: Use `$inline$` for inline formulas and `$$block$$` for multi-line formulas.
example:
Einstein: $E=mc^2$

$$\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$$

title: Mermaid diagrams
text: Create flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and more with Mermaid syntax.
example:
```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Start] --> B[End]
```

title: Export formats
text: Export your documents as PDF or DOCX — Mermaid diagrams are converted automatically.

title: Add attributes
text: Add CSS classes and IDs with `{.class #id}`.
example:
## Heading {.important #section1}

Text {style="color: red;"}

title: Pin the sidebar
text: Use the pin function to keep the sidebar always open.

title: Table of contents
text: Insert `[[toc]]` to automatically generate a table of contents.
example:
[[toc]]

## Chapter 1
## Chapter 2

title: Typographic replacements
text: Automatic conversion: `...` → …, `--` → –, `-->` → →, `(c)` → ©
example: This is a test... (c) 2026

title: Multilingual
text: The app supports German and English — switch in the settings.

title: Code with syntax highlighting
text: Use triple backticks with language: ```javascript for highlighted code.

title: Document history
text: All saved documents remain in the history — search them with the search box.

title: Links with title
text: Add tooltips to links: `[Text](url "Tooltip")`.
example: [Google](https://google.com "Search engine")

title: Startup tips
text: Enable or disable startup tips in the settings.

title: Nested blockquotes
text: Use multiple `>` for nested quotes.
example:
> Quote level 1
>> Quote level 2
>>> Quote level 3

title: Resize panes
text: Drag the divider between the editor and preview to adjust the size.

title: Create a table
text: Create tables with pipes `|` and dashes `-` for the header row.
example:
| Column 1 | Column 2 |
|----------|----------|
| Value A  | Value B  |
| Value C  | Value D  |

title: Mermaid code completion
text: Use Ctrl+Space in the editor for Mermaid command auto-completion.

title: Custom preview styles
text: Define your own CSS styles in the settings under 'Custom Styles' to tailor preview and export.

title: Mermaid: Flowchart
text: Create flowcharts with `graph TD` (top-down) or `graph LR` (left-right). Nodes: `A[Text]`, arrows: `-->`
example:
```mermaid
graph TD
  A[Start] --> B{Decision}
  B -->|Yes| C[Action 1]
  B -->|No| D[Action 2]
```

title: Mermaid: Sequence diagram
text: Show interactions with `sequenceDiagram`. Participants with `participant`, messages with `->>` or `-->`.
example:
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant A as Alice
  participant B as Bob
  A->>B: Hello!
  B-->>A: Hi!
```

title: Mermaid: Gantt chart
text: Plan projects with `gantt`. Define tasks with `section`, time spans with `:start date, duration`.
example:
```mermaid
gantt
  title Project plan
  section Phase 1
  Task 1 :a1, 2026-01-01, 30d
  Task 2 :after a1, 20d
```

title: Mermaid: Class diagram
text: Model classes with `classDiagram`. Classes: `class Name`, relationships: `<|--` (inheritance), `-->` (association).

title: Mermaid: State diagram
text: Show state transitions with `stateDiagram-v2`. States: `state Name`, transitions: `-->`.
example:
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  direction LR
  [*] --> Idle
  Idle --> Active: Start
  Active --> Idle: Stop
  Idle --> [*]
```

title: Mermaid: ER diagram
text: Model entities with `erDiagram`. Relationships: `||--o{` (1:n), `}o--o{` (n:m).

title: Mermaid: Pie chart
text: Create pie charts with `pie`. Format: `"Label" : value`.
example:
```mermaid
pie title Distribution
  "Category A" : 42
  "Category B" : 35
  "Category C" : 23
```

title: Mermaid: Git graph
text: Visualize Git branching with `gitGraph`. Commands: `commit`, `branch`, `merge`, `checkout`.
example:
```mermaid
gitGraph
  commit
  branch develop
  checkout develop
  commit
  checkout main
  merge develop
```

title: Mermaid: User journey
text: Show user flows with `journey`. Steps: `section Name`, tasks with rating 1-5.
example:
```mermaid
journey
  title Customer buys product
  section Search
    Visit website: 5: Customer
    Find product: 3: Customer
  section Purchase
    Add to cart: 4: Customer
    Pay: 2: Customer
```

title: Mermaid: Mindmap
text: Create mindmaps with `mindmap`. Hierarchy by indentation, nodes with text.
example:
```mermaid
mindmap
  root((Project))
    Planning
      Tasks
      Timeline
    Development
      Frontend
      Backend
```

title: Disable tips
text: You can disable tips in the settings if you no longer want to see them.

title: Expand sidebar
text: Click the sidebar to expand it and see your document history. Click again to minimize it.

title: Reorder files
text: Use drag & drop in the sidebar to change the order of your documents.

title: Delete files
text: Hover over a document in the history and click the delete X to remove it.

title: Outline via double-click
text: Double-click a document card in the sidebar to show the outline (table of contents).

title: Organize folders
text: Click the folder name above the history to open the folders overview and move documents between folders.

title: Switch between folders
text: Use Ctrl+↑ and Ctrl+↓ to quickly switch between your folders.

title: Sort documents in folders
text: In the folders overview you can drag and drop to reorder documents within a folder or move them between folders.

title: Edit folder names
text: Double-click a folder name in the overview to edit it inline. The default folder cannot be renamed.

title: Create a new folder
text: Click the '+' icon in the folders overview to create a new folder to organize your documents.

title: Quick open AI assistant
text: Press Alt+Space to open or close the AI assistant. If your browser or Linux window manager opens a menu instead, use Ctrl+Space as a fallback. Inside Mermaid code blocks, Ctrl+Space stays reserved for auto-completion.

title: Control document layouts globally
text: In settings you can allow or disable document-local layout blocks globally. When disabled, the Document option also disappears from layout selectors.

title: Default layout for documents
text: Choose whether new document layouts should start from Scientific, Compact, or Literary defaults.

title: Hide the layout block
text: You can hide the layout block in the editor while document-local layouts are allowed, keeping the Markdown cleaner without losing print rules.

title: Help & tips via keyboard
text: Press **F1** to open the help page, and **F2** to see a new tip at any time — even if the startup tip is disabled.

title: Layout editor via keyboard
text: Open the layout editor directly with **Ctrl+Shift+L** without touching the mouse.

title: AI assistant via keyboard
text: **Alt+Space** opens or closes the AI assistant instantly. If that opens the browser or window menu on your system, use **Ctrl+Space** instead. Inside Mermaid code blocks, **Ctrl+Space** stays reserved for auto-completion.

title: Folders overview via keyboard
text: **Ctrl+0** opens the folders overview where you can move documents between folders.

title: Visual layout editor
text: The layout editor (palette icon in preview, or Ctrl+Shift+L) lets you set page size, fonts, margins, and columns with a few clicks.

title: Real-time collaboration
text: Share a document via a collaboration link and write together with others. Cursors and changes are synchronized in real time.

title: Image alignment and width
text: Use `<!-- img: align=right width=40% -->` directly before an image to control alignment (`left`, `right`, `center`) and width. Right-aligned images are wrapped by text.
example:
<!-- img: align=right width=45% -->
![Figure 1: My diagram](image.png)

This text flows to the left of the image.

title: Auto-numbered figure captions
text: If an image's alt text starts with `Figure N:`, it is automatically numbered and receives a figure caption. The entry also appears in the list of figures.
example: ![Figure 1: System overview with data pipeline](schema.png)

title: Image frames and visual effects
text: Image markers support `frame` for a subtle border, `shadow` for a drop shadow, and `filter=grayscale` or `filter=sepia` for color effects.
example:
<!-- img: align=center width=70% frame shadow -->
![Figure 2: With frame and shadow](image.png)

title: Insert a list of figures
text: Use `<!-- list-of-figures -->` at the desired location to automatically generate a list of all numbered figures.
example:
<!-- list-of-figures -->

# List of Figures

title: Insert a list of tables
text: Use `<!-- list-of-tables -->` to generate a list of tables. Tables are automatically detected when preceded by a paragraph like `Table 1: My table`.
example:
**Table 1: Layout overview.**

| Area | Status |
|---|---|
| Margins | ☑ |

title: Auto-detected table captions
text: A paragraph immediately before a table that begins with `Table N:` is automatically recognized as a table caption, numbered, and added to the list of tables.
example:
Table 2: Comparison of layout options.

| Option | Value |
|---|---|
| Margin | 2.1 cm |

title: Automatic section numbering
text: Add `number-sections: true` to the YAML frontmatter to automatically number headings in decimal form (1, 1.1, 1.1.1 …).
example:
---
number-sections: true
---

## Introduction

### Background

title: Section IDs and cross-references
text: Assign a typed ID to a heading with `{#sec:id}` and reference it in the text with `[@sec:id]`. The preview resolves the reference into a clickable link, e.g. *Section 2.1*.
example:
## Methodology {#sec:methodology}

As described in [@sec:methodology] …

title: Semantic figure IDs and cross-references
text: Assign a typed ID to a figure using `{#fig:id}` directly after the image or with an `<!-- img: #fig:id -->` marker. Reference it in the text with `[@fig:id]` — the preview turns this into a clickable link, e.g. *Figure 2*.
example:
<!-- img: #fig:schema align=center -->

![System overview](schema.png)

As shown in [@fig:schema] …

title: Semantic table IDs and cross-references
text: Assign a typed ID to a table using `<!-- tbl: #tbl:id -->` directly before the table caption. Reference it in the text with `[@tbl:id]` — the preview turns this into a clickable link, e.g. *Table 1*.
example:
<!-- tbl: #tbl:results -->
Table 1: Measurement results overview.

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Accuracy | 94 % |

See [@tbl:results] for details.

title: Citations and bibliography
text: Set `citation-source: embedded` in the frontmatter and place BibTeX entries in a fenced code block at the end of the document. Citations like `[@Key2024]` are resolved automatically.
example:
---
citation-source: embedded
---

As shown by [@Author2024] …

``` bibtex
@article{Author2024,
  author = {Author, A.},
  title  = {Title},
  year   = {2024}
}
```

title: Bibliography panel
text: Documents with embedded sources show a book icon in the editor header. Click it to open the bibliography panel with an overview of all sources. From there you can add, edit, delete, and insert sources at the cursor position.
example:
---
citation-source: embedded
---

See [@Mueller2024].

title: Collapse the bibliography block in the editor
text: A large `mdedit-bibliography` block can take up a lot of space in the editor. In settings (Editor tab) you can collapse it to a single placeholder line — the content is preserved and the block remains accessible via the bibliography panel.

title: Scientific containers (Theorem, Definition, Proof)
text: Use `:::theorem`, `:::definition`, `:::proof`, `:::remark`, and `:::example` to create semantic containers. An optional title can follow the type: `:::theorem Mean Value Theorem`. Containers are automatically numbered and visually styled in preview and PDF.
example:
:::definition Uniform Continuity

A function $f$ is uniformly continuous on $I$ if...

:::

title: Appendix mode
text: Place `<!-- appendix -->` anywhere in your document to switch all following `##` headings into appendix sections labeled A, B, C … In the PDF each appendix starts on a new page.
example:
## Conclusion

<!-- appendix -->

## Raw Data

(Content of Appendix A)

title: Author comments (hidden in export)
text: Text between `%%` and `%%` is completely stripped from preview and export. Ideal for notes, TODOs, and editorial remarks that should not appear in the final document.
example:
%% TODO: verify sources %%

This paragraph appears normally.

title: Mermaid diagrams with caption and ID
text: A paragraph directly before a Mermaid block in the format `Figure N: Title {#fig:id}` is detected as a figure caption. The diagram is wrapped in a `<figure>` element with automatic numbering and can be referenced via `@fig:id`.
example:
Figure 1: System overview {#fig:overview}

```mermaid
graph LR
  A --> B
```

title: Embed bibliography directly in the document
text: With `citation-source: embedded` in the frontmatter, mdedit.io reads the bibliography from a `mdedit-bibliography` block inside the document. The block accepts BibTeX or CSL JSON. This keeps the bibliography part of the Markdown file — no separate .bib file needed.
example:
---
citation-source: embedded
---

See [@doe2024].

```mdedit-bibliography
@book{doe2024,
  author={Doe, Jane},
  title={Example},
  year={2024}
}
```

title: DOI lookup: fetch metadata automatically
text: Click 'DOI search' in the bibliography modal and enter a DOI (e.g. `10.1000/xyz`). mdedit.io fetches the metadata from Crossref and creates the entry automatically — no manual typing required.
example:
DOI: 10.1038/s41586-020-2649-2
→ Entry imported automatically from Crossref.

title: Zotero connector: import from your library
text: Click 'Zotero' in the bibliography modal, enter your Zotero user ID and API key, and search for sources. Selected results are added directly to the embedded block. Credentials stay in the browser (sessionStorage only) and are never sent to the server.
example:
Zotero User ID: 12345678
API Key: AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWx
→ Search and import from your Zotero library.

title: Import and export bibliography
text: In the bibliography modal you can import .bib (BibTeX) and .json (CSL JSON) files — duplicate keys are skipped. Use the 'JSON' and 'BibTeX' buttons to download the current block as a file.
example:
Import: existing.bib → entries are merged
Export JSON: bibliography.json
Export BibTeX: bibliography.bib
